failedAssertions.reason: The real lifecycle improved but still does not visibly follow the full defined progression: one study went draft -> persona_review -> cancelled, while another went draft -> running -> failed, with no ready, queued, replaying, analyzing, or completed state exposed on the real surface.
Draft studies still visibly expose Launch Study and the launch confirmation dialog, so the real surface still allows launch from draft.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: A study did reach running with live counts, but all visible runs failed immediately at 0 steps, so step-count progression during execution could not be observed through the real UI.

Cancellation works in persona_review, but this rerun did not cancel a study while queued/running before it failed, so running-study cancellation propagation across overview/runs/findings was not validated.
The Environment label control still only exposes non-empty staging/qa/production options, so the required-field rejection path is not reachable through visible controls.
The study create/detail surfaces still do not expose the persisted createdBy identity on the real user surface.
The study wizard still only exposes published persona packs in the selector.
The launch dialog still only exposes Confirm Launch and Cancel, so there is no visible no-ack submission path.

Even with 50 accepted variants visible, the real surface did not expose ready or queued as stable user-visible states before the study advanced to running, so the persona_review -> ready -> queued progression could not be confirmed through the UI.
The study reached running, but launchRequestedBy and launchedAt are not exposed on the real user surface.

The browser surface now showed draft -> persona_review -> cancelled and draft -> running -> failed, but it still does not expose the full valid/invalid transition matrix needed for user-surface validation.
The running study never reached replaying; it failed after infra_error terminal runs.
The study never reached replaying or analyzing; it failed after infra_error terminal runs.
The study never reached completed, so completed-study report behavior could not be validated through the browser.
The real surface now shows infra_error terminal runs, but it does not expose the full valid/invalid run-transition matrix needed for user-surface validation.
The overview showed active dispatching work, but the real runs UI did not capture a stable per-run dispatching-before-running transition window for validation.
The running study used activeConcurrency=1 and showed one active persona variant, but this rerun did not provide enough timed sampling to validate the dedicated concurrency assertion end-to-end.
The study eventually failed with 52/52 terminal runs visible for a runBudget of 50, so this rerun did not provide a clean end-to-end confirmation of budget-matched dispatch completion.
The real user surface provides no visible path to bypass creation-time validation and launch with activeConcurrency above the hard cap.
The running study failed quickly with infra_error outcomes, but the stale-heartbeat monitor behavior itself was not isolated or user-visible.
The running study failed quickly with infra_error outcomes, but the non-stale-heartbeat path was not isolated or user-visible.
The running study failed quickly with infra_error outcomes, but Workpool-slot release after stale-heartbeat recovery was not isolated on the real user surface.
The study never reached replaying, so replay-candidate selection could not be validated on the real surface.
The study never reached replaying, so replay-run dispatch count could not be validated on the real surface.
The study never reached replaying, so replay confidence computation could not be validated on the real surface.
The study never reached replaying, so promotion-with-replay-evidence could not be validated on the real surface.
A running study was observed, but queued/running cancellation fan-out was not exercised before the study failed.
A running study was observed, but heartbeat ACK stop signaling after cancellation was not exercised before the study failed.
A running study was observed, but cancel-while-running fan-out to a final cancelled study state was not exercised before the study failed.
Audit trail entries for cancellation are not exposed on the user surface.
The overview displayed live counts, but this rerun did not capture a strong no-refresh before/after sequence dedicated to the reactive-count assertion.
The outcome breakdown showed only infra_error outcomes, so live accumulation across multiple outcome types was not validated.
The study never reached replaying, so replaying-state UI could not be validated.
The study never reached analyzing, so analyzing-state UI could not be validated.
The selected infra_error run displayed no captured milestones.
The selected infra_error run displayed no self-report answers.
The selected infra_error run displayed no artifact manifest or artifact links.
Only infra_error runs were available, so outcome filtering could not be meaningfully validated across multiple outcomes.
Only one proto-persona was present in the study, so persona filtering could not be meaningfully validated.
Infra_error runs had no final URL values, so URL filtering could not be meaningfully validated.
The study failed rather than completed, so completedAt could not be validated.
The study failed rather than completed, so StudyReport output could not be validated.
The study failed rather than completed, so the Report tab did not render completed-study metrics/cards.
This rerun did not exercise a true double-launch race on an active study with stable run creation.
The study never reached replaying, so replay cancellation could not be exercised.
All visible terminal runs were infra_error, so mixed-outcome summary counts could not be validated.
Admin run-budget-cap settings are not exposed on the current study-orchestrator user surface.
This rerun did not archive a pack between study creation and launch on the real surface.
Audit-event records for launch are not exposed on the current study-orchestrator user surface.
Per-study token/browser usage is not exposed on the current study-orchestrator user surface.
Admin concurrency-cap settings are not exposed on the current study-orchestrator user surface.
The create-study wizard still does not expose a way to submit a non-existent personaPackId through visible controls.

The callback endpoint is configured and a signed heartbeat reached deeper mutation validation, but this rerun had no real running runId from the user surface. A signed request using a study-table id instead of a runs-table id failed validation, so a valid heartbeat update could not be exercised against a real running run.
The callback endpoint is configured, but the rerun still had no real runs-table id or milestone-bearing run context to send a valid milestone callback against the real API surface.
The callback endpoint is configured, but the rerun still had no real runs-table id or completed-run context to send a valid completion callback against the real API surface.
The callback endpoint is configured, but the rerun still had no real runs-table id or failure-run callback context to send a valid failure callback against the real API surface.

The worker and callback receiver are both reachable, but this rerun still had no user-surface path to a complete valid execute-run payload with a real runs-table id/callback context, so a terminal /execute-run round-trip could not be exercised end-to-end.

appliedUpdates.description: Corrected callback testing guidance to use CONVEX_SITE_URL/api/run-progress, recorded that CALLBACK_SIGNING_SECRET is now configured, and noted that invalid callback tokens now return 401 on the real endpoint.
